user_query:
I want an event-driven helper for my podcast/blog workflow. Please build a small automation that watches the local folder "workspace/drafts" for Markdown (.md) files that start with YAML front matter containing both keys: feast and tradition. On startup, process any .md files already in that folder once, then keep watching for newly created .md files and process them as they appear.

For each qualifying draft:
1) Inspect the file and directory to derive a slug from the filename (base name without extension, lowercase). Use this slug consistently for all outputs related to that draft.
2) Parse the YAML front matter to read feast and tradition.
3) Perform live web search queries to locate two sources:
   - Query A: an official liturgical description for the given feast in the specified tradition. Choose a result from an official domain for that tradition when possible:
     • Roman Catholic: prefer domains such as usccb.org or vatican.va
     • Anglican/Episcopal: prefer episcopalchurch.org or churchofengland.org
     • Eastern Orthodox: prefer goarch.org, oca.org, or antiochian.org
     If no such domain appears in results, pick the top non-advertised result that is not a forum or social network.
   - Query B: a reputable iconography/symbolism resource for the feast (e.g., an educational or museum site). Prefer domains containing indicators like edu or museum (e.g., metmuseum, britishmuseum, getty.edu). If none appear, pick the top non-advertised encyclopedia or academic resource.
   Note: Do not hardcode URLs; acquire them via live Internet search.
4) Download each chosen page’s HTML to "workspace/sources/{slug}/1.html" and "workspace/sources/{slug}/2.html".
5) Extract the page title and the first non-empty paragraph of text from each downloaded HTML. Compute the word count of that first paragraph.
6) Save a machine-readable summary at "workspace/sources/{slug}/digest.json" with this structure:
   {
     "feast": string,
     "tradition": string,
     "queries": ["query for A", "query for B"],
     "sources": [
       {
         "resolved_url": string,
         "domain": string,
         "page_title": string,
         "first_paragraph": string,
         "first_paragraph_word_count": number
       },
       { ... second source ... }
     ]
   }
7) Generate show notes at "workspace/show_notes/{slug}.md" with:
   - Title line: "# Show Notes: {feast}"
   - A line: "Tradition: {tradition}"
   - A "Sources" section listing each source’s page_title and resolved_url
   - A "Key excerpts" section containing the first 200 characters from each source’s first_paragraph
8) Append a single-line entry to "workspace/logs/activity.log" summarizing the processed draft (absolute path), slug, number of sources saved, and an ISO-8601 timestamp.

Requirements and constraints:
- Only process files that contain both feast and tradition in YAML front matter; ignore others.
- If "workspace/show_notes/{slug}.md" already exists, skip re-downloading and do not duplicate work for that draft.
- Use UTF-8 for all outputs.
- The automation should be runnable locally without credentials and rely on public search; the chosen engine is up to you.

Please implement this so that when I run it once in my workspace, it processes the included sample draft and produces all artifacts under "workspace/" as specified. After the initial run, it should continue watching for new drafts until I stop it. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
